([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Oost-Vlaanderen* [[ˌoːst ˈflaːndərə(n)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Oost-Vlaanderen.ogg "File:Nl-Oost-Vlaanderen.ogg");[[a]](#cite_note-5) [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Flandre-Orientale* [[flɑ̃dʁ ɔʁjɑ̃tal]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Flandre-Orientale-FR.wav "File:Flandre-Orientale-FR.wav"); [German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Ostflandern* [[ˈɔstˌflandɐn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:De-Ostflandern.ogg "File:De-Ostflandern.ogg"); [West Flemish](/wiki/West_Flemish_language "West Flemish language"): *Ôost-Vloandern*) is a [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Belgium "Provinces of Belgium") of [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"). It borders (clockwise from the North) the [Dutch](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands") province of [Zeeland](/wiki/Zeeland "Zeeland") and the Belgian provinces of [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp_(province) "Antwerp (province)"), [Flemish Brabant](/wiki/Flemish_Brabant "Flemish Brabant"), [Hainaut](/wiki/Hainaut_(province) "Hainaut (province)") and [West Flanders](/wiki/West_Flanders "West Flanders"). It has an area of 3,007 km2 (1,161 sq mi), divided into six administrative districts containing 60 municipalities, and a population of over 1.57 million as of January 2024.[[5]](#cite_note-6) The capital is [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), home to the [Ghent University](/wiki/Ghent_University "Ghent University") and the [Port of Ghent](/wiki/Port_of_Ghent "Port of Ghent").

**[NUTS](/wiki/Nomenclature_of_Territorial_Units_for_Statistics "Nomenclature of Territorial Units for Statistics")**   **[NIS](/wiki/Statistics_Belgium "Statistics Belgium")**   **Population**   **Area** | BE.OV.GT  BE234  44  553,961  944 km² | BE.OV.OD  BE235  45  123,330  419 km² | BE.OV.EK  BE233  43  84,113  334 km² | BE.OV.AL  BE231  41  286,741  469 km² | BE.OV.DM  BE232  42  199,553  343 km² | BE.OV.SN  BE236  46  248,489  475 km² |

**East Flanders** ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Oost-Vlaanderen* [[ˌoːst ˈflaːndərə(n)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Oost-Vlaanderen.ogg "File:Nl-Oost-Vlaanderen.ogg");[[a]](#cite_note-5) [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Flandre-Orientale* [[flɑ̃dʁ ɔʁjɑ̃tal]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Flandre-Orientale-FR.wav "File:Flandre-Orientale-FR.wav"); [German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Ostflandern* [[ˈɔstˌflandɐn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:De-Ostflandern.ogg "File:De-Ostflandern.ogg"); [West Flemish](/wiki/West_Flemish_language "West Flemish language"): *Ôost-Vloandern*) is a [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Belgium "Provinces of Belgium") of [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"). It borders (clockwise from the North) the [Dutch](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands") province of [Zeeland](/wiki/Zeeland "Zeeland") and the Belgian provinces of [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp_(province) "Antwerp (province)"), [Flemish Brabant](/wiki/Flemish_Brabant "Flemish Brabant"), [Hainaut](/wiki/Hainaut_(province) "Hainaut (province)") and [West Flanders](/wiki/West_Flanders "West Flanders"). It has an area of 3,007 km2 (1,161 sq mi), divided into six administrative districts containing 60 municipalities, and a population of over 1.57 million as of January 2024.[[5]](#cite_note-6) The capital is [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), home to the [Ghent University](/wiki/Ghent_University "Ghent University") and the [Port of Ghent](/wiki/Port_of_Ghent "Port of Ghent").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Flanders&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

During the short-lived [Napoleonic Empire](/wiki/Napoleonic_Empire "Napoleonic Empire"), most of the area of the modern province was part of the [Department of Escaut](/wiki/Escaut_(department) "Escaut (department)"), named after the [River Scheldt](/wiki/River_Scheldt "River Scheldt"). Following the defeat of [Napoleon](/wiki/Napoleon "Napoleon"), the entity was [renamed](/wiki/Geographical_renaming "Geographical renaming") after its geographical location in the eastern part of the historic [County of Flanders](/wiki/County_of_Flanders "County of Flanders") (now in the western portion of the current [Flemish Region](/wiki/Flemish_Region "Flemish Region")).

The provincial flag has a black lion with red tongue and claws, on a background of horizontal white and green stripes. This is a recent adaptation; formerly, East Flanders used the [Flemish flag](/wiki/Flag_of_Flanders "Flag of Flanders"), a black lion on a yellow background, as in the current coat of arms. The old flag is still publicly used, e.g. for road signs.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Flanders&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

The province has several geographic or tourist regions:

* [Denderstreek](/wiki/Denderstreek "Denderstreek")
* [Meetjesland](/wiki/Meetjesland "Meetjesland")
* [Waasland](/wiki/Waasland "Waasland")
* [Flemish Ardennes](/wiki/Flemish_Ardennes "Flemish Ardennes")

Important rivers are the [Scheldt](/wiki/Scheldt "Scheldt") and the [Leie](/wiki/Leie "Leie") which merge in [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"). The [Dender](/wiki/Dender "Dender") merges into the [Scheldt](/wiki/Scheldt "Scheldt") in the city of [Dendermonde](/wiki/Dendermonde "Dendermonde").

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Flanders&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Subdivisions")]

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Flanders&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demographics")]

The province has a population of almost 1.5 million. It had 734,000 inhabitants in 1830, when it was the most populated province of Belgium, and about a million in 1900. Population growth halted around the 1980s, but has increased again in the 21st century. Population figures in recent years is as follows:

| Year | Population |
| --- | --- |
| 1 January 1990 | 1,331,608 |
| 1 January 1995 | 1,349,382 |
| 1 January 2000 | 1,361,623 |
| 1 January 2005 | 1,380,072 |
| 1 January 2010 | 1,432,326 |
| 1 January 2015 | 1,477,346 |
| 1 January 2017 | 1,496,187 |

The capital and biggest city is [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), also the second largest city in the [Flemish Region](/wiki/Flemish_Region "Flemish Region"). Other smaller cities are [Aalst](/wiki/Aalst,_Belgium "Aalst, Belgium"), [Sint-Niklaas](/wiki/Sint-Niklaas "Sint-Niklaas") and [Dendermonde](/wiki/Dendermonde "Dendermonde") in the east of the province. The eastern part of the province, part of the [Flemish Diamond](/wiki/Flemish_Diamond "Flemish Diamond"), is more densely populated than the western part.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Flanders&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Economy")]

The [Gross domestic product](/wiki/Gross_domestic_product "Gross domestic product") (GDP) of the province was 56.4 billion € in 2018. GDP per capita adjusted for purchasing power was 33,000 € or 109% of the EU27 average in the same year.[[6]](#cite_note-7)

## Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Flanders&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Government")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f5/Gravensteengent22042008.JPG/250px-Gravensteengent22042008.JPG)](/wiki/File:Gravensteengent22042008.JPG)

The *[Gravensteen](/wiki/Gravensteen "Gravensteen")* in [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), the provincial capital

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9c/Geraardsbergen_-_Oudeberg_top_2.jpg/250px-Geraardsbergen_-_Oudeberg_top_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Geraardsbergen_-_Oudeberg_top_2.jpg)

The *[Muur van Geraardsbergen](/wiki/Muur_van_Geraardsbergen "Muur van Geraardsbergen")*

The provincial council (*provincieraad*) consists of 36 members which were last elected in the [2018 elections](/wiki/2018_Belgian_local_elections#Provincial_elections "2018 Belgian local elections"). Previously it consisted of 72 members. The council currently consists of the following political parties:

* [N-VA](/wiki/N-VA "N-VA") (Flemish nationalists): 8 members
* [CD&V](/wiki/CD%26V "CD&V") (Christian democrats): 7 members
* [Open VLD](/wiki/Open_VLD "Open VLD") (liberals): 7 members
* [sp.a](/wiki/Sp.a "Sp.a") (social democrats): 3 members
* [Vlaams Belang](/wiki/Vlaams_Belang "Vlaams Belang") (far-right nationalists): 6 members
* [Groen](/wiki/Groen! "Groen!") (greens): 5 members

Four people chosen by and from the council form the daily government, called the deputation (*deputatie*). The deputation of East Flanders is a coalition of the [N-VA](/wiki/N-VA "N-VA"), [CD&V](/wiki/CD%26V "CD&V"), and [Groen](/wiki/Open_Vld "Open Vld").

The daily government is led by the governor, who is appointed by the [Flemish Government](/wiki/Flemish_Government "Flemish Government"). [Carina Van Cauter](/wiki/Carina_Van_Cauter "Carina Van Cauter") ([VLD](/wiki/Open_Vlaamse_Liberalen_en_Democraten "Open Vlaamse Liberalen en Democraten")) has been the governor of East Flanders since 1 September 2020.[[7]](#cite_note-8)

The province has a yearly budget of approximately 300 million euro.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Flanders&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-5)** In isolation, *Vlaanderen* is pronounced [[ˈvlaːndərə(n)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch").
